allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2021-0732
allVulnerabilities.description:
XStream, when in its default configuration or operating without a sufficient blacklist / whitelist configuration, is vulnerable to an arbitrary file deletion issue. This is due to how XStream can potentially create new instances of dangerous objects that are not blocked based on the contents of the input stream. The potentially dangerous types in this case are  `sun.awt.datatransfer.DataTransferer$IndexOrderComparator` and `java.io.FileInputStream`.

An attacker could supply a crafted input to XStream in order deletion arbitrary files on the local host.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**1.4.16**](https://github.com/x-stream/xstream/releases/tag/XSTREAM_1_4_16) by [this](https://github.com/x-stream/xstream/commit/d5e51177634afea7213b9dc2d21f101d2e258db9) commit.

The latest stable releases are available [here](http://x-stream.github.io/download.html).
allVulnerabilities.workaround:
The vendor recommends the use of XStream's security framework to implement a whitelist for allowed types. 

The vendor has made available a set of [workarounds](https://x-stream.github.io/security.html#workaround) detailing this feature.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2024-8322

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2022-3693
allVulnerabilities.description: XStream is vulnerable to denial-of-service due to a stack overflow that exists due to a recursive hash set calculation. A remote attacker could cause a vulnerable application to crash by causing that application to process a maliciously crafted input stream.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in version [**1.4.20**](https://github.com/x-stream/xstream/releases/tag/XSTREAM_1_4_20) by [this](https://github.com/x-stream/xstream/commit/e9151f221b4969fb15b1e946d5d61dcdd459a391) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/x-stream/xstream/tags).
allVulnerabilities.workaround:
The vendor has stated [here](https://github.com/x-stream/xstream/security/advisories/GHSA-j563-grx4-pjpv) that the following workaround can be implemented:

> The attack uses the hash code implementation for collections and maps to force recursive hash calculation causing a stack overflow. Following types of the Java runtime are affected:
>
> * java.util.HashMap
> * java.util.HashSet
> * java.util.Hashtable
> * java.util.LinkedHashMap
> * java.util.LinkedHashSet
> Other third party collection implementations that use their element's hash code may also be affected
> A simple solution is to catch the StackOverflowError in the client code calling XStream.
> 
> If your object graph does not use referenced elements at all, you may simply set the NO_REFERENCE mode:
> 
> XStream xstream = new XStream();
> xstream.setMode(XStream.NO_REFERENCES);
> If your object graph contains neither a Hashtable, HashMap nor a HashSet (or one of the linked variants of it) then you can use the security framework to deny the usage of these types:
> 
> XStream xstream = new XStream();
> xstream.denyTypes(new Class[]{
>  java.util.HashMap.class, java.util.HashSet.class, java.util.Hashtable.class, java.util.LinkedHashMap.class, java.util.LinkedHashSet.class
> });
> Unfortunately these types are very common. If you only use HashMap or HashSet and your XML refers these only as default map or set, you may additionally change the default implementation of java.util.Map and java.util.Set at unmarshalling time::
> 
> xstream.addDefaultImplementation(java.util.TreeMap.class, java.util.Map.class);
> xstream.addDefaultImplementation(java.util.TreeSet.class, java.util.Set.class);
> However, this implies that your application does not care about the implementation of the map and all elements are comparable.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2016-0027
allVulnerabilities.description:
XStream, when using some supported parsers, is vulnerable to an XML entity expansion issue due to how these parsers can process external entities by default.

A remote attacker could use this issue to retrieve the content of arbitrary files with known locations in a local file system if the Java process has read access.

**Note**: XStream is not vulnerable if the default XML Pull Parser (`Xpp3` or `kXML2`) is used. These parser types do not process XML entities.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
This vulnerability is fixed in version [**1.4.9**](http://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/com/thoughtworks/xstream/xstream/1.4.9/).

**Note**: Applications using one of the following parser implementations are still vulnerable:
* `DOM` implementation from Java 5 runtime and below
* `StAX` implementation from Java 6 runtime and below
* `StAX` implementation from BEA (old reference implementation)
* `XOM`

The latest stable releases are available [here](https://x-stream.github.io/download.html).
allVulnerabilities.workaround: The vendor has recommended [here](https://x-stream.github.io/CVE-2016-3674.html) that users can opt to use one of the XML Pull Parser implementations in order to mitigate this issue.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2021-0737

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4914
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Boot is vulnerable to authentication bypass due to improper handling of endpoint request matchers in the `EndpointRequest` component. This could allow an attacker to access protected application endpoints without proper authentication, potentially exposing sensitive functionality or data.

An application is only vulnerable when it combines Spring Boot Actuator with a specific configuration pattern: the application must have the Actuator dependency on its classpath and configure a custom health group (e.g., "mygroup") using properties like `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.include`. This health group must then be exposed under an additional path on the main server using a configuration such as `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.additional-path=server:/healthz`. The vulnerability becomes exploitable when the application also defines an endpoint requiring authentication that is located under a subpath of this additional health path (e.g., `/healthz/admin`). In this scenario, the flawed path matching logic causes the health group matcher to incorrectly match the authentication-protected endpoint, potentially allowing unauthenticated access to what should be a secured resource.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in:
* [**3.5.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v3.5.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7607
allVulnerabilities.description: Spring Framework is vulnerable to denial-of-service (DoS) due to a missing existence check in the `FileSystemResource.isReadable()` method used during static resource resolution. When serving static resources from the file system on Windows, the `isReadable()` method called `File.canRead()` without first verifying that the file exists. On Windows, `canRead()` performs expensive security descriptor lookups even for non-existent files, causing these calls to be slow to return. This could allow an attacker to send a high volume of crafted requests for non-existent static resources, keeping HTTP connections occupied and eventually exhausting available connections, resulting in a denial-of-service condition for legitimate users.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.18**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.18) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/684b1e8a4b3df9589f3e9abb8c09a5e98a47e4b5) commit and in [**7.0.7**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.7) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/e977bd7bf0efe8341a03de025ee6ac376e6a57c8) commit.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7608
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring framework contains a cache poisoning vulnerability due to how static resources are resolved when using Spring MVD or Spring Webflux. A remote attacker could exploit this flaw by tricking a victim into interacting with poisoned content, which could lead to a denial-of-service (DoS) and impacts to application performance.

To be vulnerable to this flaw, an application must meet the following conditions:

* the application is using Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux,
* the application is configuring the resource chain support with caching enabled,
* the application adds support for encoded resources resolution,
* the resource cache must be empty when the attacker has access to the application.
allVulnerabilities.solution:

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4886
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework MVC and WebFlux are vulnerable to stream corruption due to improper handling of server-sent events (SSE).

An application is vulnerable when using Spring MVC's `SseEmitter` or Spring WebFlux's `ServerSentEvent` to send Server-Sent Events (SSE) to clients, and an attacker has control over values used in the SSE id or event fields. By injecting newline characters (`\n`, `\r`, or `\r\n`) into these fields, an attacker can break out of the intended SSE field and inject arbitrary SSE events (such as malicious data:, event:, or id: fields) into the stream. Plain text strings in the data field containing CR/CRLF characters are vulnerable but JSON data would be safe in the datafield as the JSON parser would would escape all newline variants properly.
This could allow the attacker to corrupt the SSE stream received by other users, potentially altering application state or presenting false information to victims.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.17**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.17) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/8dc888e1b8190da93d0864c437ae945eaf06a8e9) commit, and [**7.0.6**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.6) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/6e9758700a4946be1dca85ca937ef2603e291301) commit.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4886
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework MVC and WebFlux are vulnerable to stream corruption due to improper handling of server-sent events (SSE).

An application is vulnerable when using Spring MVC's `SseEmitter` or Spring WebFlux's `ServerSentEvent` to send Server-Sent Events (SSE) to clients, and an attacker has control over values used in the SSE id or event fields. By injecting newline characters (`\n`, `\r`, or `\r\n`) into these fields, an attacker can break out of the intended SSE field and inject arbitrary SSE events (such as malicious data:, event:, or id: fields) into the stream. Plain text strings in the data field containing CR/CRLF characters are vulnerable but JSON data would be safe in the datafield as the JSON parser would would escape all newline variants properly.
This could allow the attacker to corrupt the SSE stream received by other users, potentially altering application state or presenting false information to victims.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.17**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.17) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/8dc888e1b8190da93d0864c437ae945eaf06a8e9) commit, and [**7.0.6**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.6) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/6e9758700a4946be1dca85ca937ef2603e291301) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4873
allVulnerabilities.description:

**Note**: In order for the application to be vulnerable it must have a mapping for `/**` that results in view rendering where the view name is not explicitly specified.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7597

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7607
allVulnerabilities.description: Spring Framework is vulnerable to denial-of-service (DoS) due to a missing existence check in the `FileSystemResource.isReadable()` method used during static resource resolution. When serving static resources from the file system on Windows, the `isReadable()` method called `File.canRead()` without first verifying that the file exists. On Windows, `canRead()` performs expensive security descriptor lookups even for non-existent files, causing these calls to be slow to return. This could allow an attacker to send a high volume of crafted requests for non-existent static resources, keeping HTTP connections occupied and eventually exhausting available connections, resulting in a denial-of-service condition for legitimate users.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.18**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.18) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/684b1e8a4b3df9589f3e9abb8c09a5e98a47e4b5) commit and in [**7.0.7**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.7) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/e977bd7bf0efe8341a03de025ee6ac376e6a57c8) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2025-13947
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework is vulnerable to a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) issue due to the presence of a security bypass flaw in the STOMP protocol functionality. Applications that use STOMP over WebSocket are affected by this issue.

An attacker could trick a victim user into viewing crafted web content that sends requests to an affected application and abuses improper state management to bypass security measures and send unauthorized messages on the victim user's behalf.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in Spring Framework OSS [**7.0.0-RC1**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.0-RC1) and [**6.2.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/c88bfc54c9256e3c07511aa303be2b136c03e0e2) commit.

The latest stable releases are available [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7608
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring framework contains a cache poisoning vulnerability due to how static resources are resolved when using Spring MVD or Spring Webflux. A remote attacker could exploit this flaw by tricking a victim into interacting with poisoned content, which could lead to a denial-of-service (DoS) and impacts to application performance.

To be vulnerable to this flaw, an application must meet the following conditions:

* the application is using Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux,
* the application is configuring the resource chain support with caching enabled,
* the application adds support for encoded resources resolution,
* the resource cache must be empty when the attacker has access to the application.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.18**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.18) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/e607f1c30fd99e146cdaf186c56d1f1275e3fbc1) commit, and [**7.0.7**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.7) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/0b2e3900b9ce57873f3393ba9e026cb58d8721ba) commit.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4925
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Boot is vulnerable to authentication bypass due to improper handling of requests in the `CloudFoundry` actuator endpoint mapping. This could allow an attacker to access protected application endpoints without proper authentication.

**Note:** A Spring Boot application is only vulnerable when both the Actuator and Spring Security dependencies are on the classpath. The application must have a custom endpoint that requires authentication mapped under the `CloudFoundry` actuator path (such as `/cloudfoundryapplication/admin`). This specific misconfiguration of placing application endpoints under the infrastructure actuator namespace is the key prerequisite for this vulnerability to manifest.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4914
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Boot is vulnerable to authentication bypass due to improper handling of endpoint request matchers in the `EndpointRequest` component. This could allow an attacker to access protected application endpoints without proper authentication, potentially exposing sensitive functionality or data.

An application is only vulnerable when it combines Spring Boot Actuator with a specific configuration pattern: the application must have the Actuator dependency on its classpath and configure a custom health group (e.g., "mygroup") using properties like `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.include`. This health group must then be exposed under an additional path on the main server using a configuration such as `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.additional-path=server:/healthz`. The vulnerability becomes exploitable when the application also defines an endpoint requiring authentication that is located under a subpath of this additional health path (e.g., `/healthz/admin`). In this scenario, the flawed path matching logic causes the health group matcher to incorrectly match the authentication-protected endpoint, potentially allowing unauthenticated access to what should be a secured resource.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in:
* [**3.5.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v3.5.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit

* [**4.1.0-M3**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v4.1.0-M3) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit

This health group must then be exposed under an additional path on the main server using a configuration such as `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.additional-path=server:/healthz`. The vulnerability becomes exploitable when the application also defines an endpoint requiring authentication that is located under a subpath of this additional health path (e.g., `/healthz/admin`). In this scenario, the flawed path matching logic causes the health group matcher to incorrectly match the authentication-protected endpoint, potentially allowing unauthenticated access to what should be a secured resource.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in:
* [**3.5.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v3.5.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit
* [**4.0.4**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v4.0.4) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit
* [**4.1.0-M3**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v4.1.0-M3) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7849
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Security is vulnerable to an information disclosure issue when `DaoAuthenticationProvider` is in use as a result of how timing attack defenses can potentially be bypassed.

Specifically, where an application is using the `UserDetails#isEnabled`, `#isAccountNonExpired` or `#isAccountNonLocked` user attributes, defenses can be bypassed for users who are disabled, expired or locked. Authentication can take different lengths of time to complete depending on user account state, which leaks some user attributes.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**7.0.5**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases/tag/7.0.5) and in [**6.5.10**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases/tag/6.5.10) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/commit/a317a3d8663995daa2bc91593bb88ae2da21ea52) commit.

The latest stable releases are available [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases).

In the official [advisory](https://spring.io/security/cve-2026-22746), the vendor has provided the following advice regarding the fixes for this issue:
> Note that this version also introduces a setter `DaoAuthenticationProvider#setAlwaysPerformAdditionalChecksOnUser`. In the event that this upgrade causes you trouble, you can set this value to `false`.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7845
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Security is vulnerable to a time-of-check time-of-use (TOCTOU) race condition due to improper handling of token deletion in the `JdbcOneTimeTokenService` component. This could allow an attacker to consume a one-time token multiple times, potentially bypassing authentication mechanisms.

**Note:** The default `InMemoryOneTimeTokenService` is thread-safe and not affected by this vulnerability.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7851
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Security is susceptible to a security misconfiguration issue when using `NimbusJwtDecoder#withIssuerLocation` or `NimbusReactiveJwtDecoder#withIssuerLocation` to configure JWT decoding.

By default, the decoder only verifies the cryptographic signature of JWTs without validating the issuer, unless developers explicitly add an `OAuth2TokenValidator<Jwt>` by calling `setJwtValidator()`. This creates a dangerous security gap where applications may unknowingly accept JWTs from any source as long as they are properly signed.

This default state could lead to authentication bypass scenarios where an attacker sends fraudulent tokens to an affected application wherein they are accepted because the application only checks if the signature is valid, not whether the token came from the trusted authorization server.

This default state could lead to authentication bypass scenarios where an attacker sends fraudulent tokens to an affected application wherein they are accepted because the application only checks if the signature is valid, not whether the token came from the trusted authorization server.

allVulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-24400
allVulnerabilities.description: AssertJ provides Fluent testing assertions for Java and the Java Virtual Machine (JVM). Starting in version 1.4.0 and prior to version 3.27.7, an XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists in `org.assertj.core.util.xml.XmlStringPrettyFormatter`: the `toXmlDocument(String)` method initializes `DocumentBuilderFactory` with default settings, without disabling DTDs or external entities. This formatter is used by the `isXmlEqualTo(CharSequence)` assertion for `CharSequence` values. An application is vulnerable only when it uses untrusted XML input with either `isXmlEqualTo(CharSequence)` from `org.assertj.core.api.AbstractCharSequenceAssert` or `xmlPrettyFormat(String)` from `org.assertj.core.util.xml.XmlStringPrettyFormatter`. If untrusted XML input is processed by tone of these methods, an attacker couldnread arbitrary local files via `file://` URIs (e.g., `/etc/passwd`, application configuration files); perform Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via HTTP/HTTPS URIs, and/or cause Denial of Service via "Billion Laughs" entity expansion attacks. `isXmlEqualTo(CharSequence)` has been deprecated in favor of XMLUnit in version 3.18.0 and will be removed in version 4.0. Users of affected versions should, in order of preference: replace `isXmlEqualTo(CharSequence)` with XMLUnit, upgrade to version 3.27.7, or avoid using `isXmlEqualTo(CharSequence)` or `XmlStringPrettyFormatter` with untrusted input. `XmlStringPrettyFormatter` has historically been considered a utility for `isXmlEqualTo(CharSequence)` rather than a feature for AssertJ users, so it is deprecated in version 3.27.7 and removed in version 4.0, with no replacement.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7607
allVulnerabilities.description: Spring Framework is vulnerable to denial-of-service (DoS) due to a missing existence check in the `FileSystemResource.isReadable()` method used during static resource resolution. When serving static resources from the file system on Windows, the `isReadable()` method called `File.canRead()` without first verifying that the file exists. On Windows, `canRead()` performs expensive security descriptor lookups even for non-existent files, causing these calls to be slow to return. This could allow an attacker to send a high volume of crafted requests for non-existent static resources, keeping HTTP connections occupied and eventually exhausting available connections, resulting in a denial-of-service condition for legitimate users.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7608
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring framework contains a cache poisoning vulnerability due to how static resources are resolved when using Spring MVD or Spring Webflux. A remote attacker could exploit this flaw by tricking a victim into interacting with poisoned content, which could lead to a denial-of-service (DoS) and impacts to application performance.

To be vulnerable to this flaw, an application must meet the following conditions:

* the application is using Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux,
* the application is configuring the resource chain support with caching enabled,
* the application adds support for encoded resources resolution,
* the resource cache must be empty when the attacker has access to the application.
allVulnerabilities.solution:

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4886
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework MVC and WebFlux are vulnerable to stream corruption due to improper handling of server-sent events (SSE).

An application is vulnerable when using Spring MVC's `SseEmitter` or Spring WebFlux's `ServerSentEvent` to send Server-Sent Events (SSE) to clients, and an attacker has control over values used in the SSE id or event fields. By injecting newline characters (`\n`, `\r`, or `\r\n`) into these fields, an attacker can break out of the intended SSE field and inject arbitrary SSE events (such as malicious data:, event:, or id: fields) into the stream. Plain text strings in the data field containing CR/CRLF characters are vulnerable but JSON data would be safe in the datafield as the JSON parser would would escape all newline variants properly.
This could allow the attacker to corrupt the SSE stream received by other users, potentially altering application state or presenting false information to victims.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.17**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.17) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/8dc888e1b8190da93d0864c437ae945eaf06a8e9) commit, and [**7.0.6**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.6) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/6e9758700a4946be1dca85ca937ef2603e291301) commit.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4914
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Boot is vulnerable to authentication bypass due to improper handling of endpoint request matchers in the `EndpointRequest` component. This could allow an attacker to access protected application endpoints without proper authentication, potentially exposing sensitive functionality or data.

An application is only vulnerable when it combines Spring Boot Actuator with a specific configuration pattern: the application must have the Actuator dependency on its classpath and configure a custom health group (e.g., "mygroup") using properties like `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.include`. This health group must then be exposed under an additional path on the main server using a configuration such as `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.additional-path=server:/healthz`. The vulnerability becomes exploitable when the application also defines an endpoint requiring authentication that is located under a subpath of this additional health path (e.g., `/healthz/admin`). In this scenario, the flawed path matching logic causes the health group matcher to incorrectly match the authentication-protected endpoint, potentially allowing unauthenticated access to what should be a secured resource.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in:
* [**3.5.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v3.5.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-2347
allVulnerabilities.description:
Apache Tomcat is vulnerable to certificate verification bypass due to improper virtual host mapping in applications enforcing client certificate authentication at the Connector. An attacker could exploit this by sending different host names in the SNI extension and the HTTP host header field. Successful exploitation could allow for a client to bypass the client certificate authentication. 

**Note:** The vulnerability does not apply in settings where client certificate authentication is enforced at the web application.

**Note:** The original fix for this vulnerability was insufficient and could be bypassed. This issue was assigned **CVE-2026-32990** (**BDSA-2026-6768**).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-6774

This issue could allow a remote attacker to inject arbitrary JSON fragments into access log entries, compromising the integrity of log data. This could result in cross-site scripting or other problems in downstream parsing/display components.

**Note:** This requires the application to use non-default values for the `Connector` attributes `relaxedPathChars` and/or `relaxedQueryChars`.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4925
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Boot is vulnerable to authentication bypass due to improper handling of requests in the `CloudFoundry` actuator endpoint mapping. This could allow an attacker to access protected application endpoints without proper authentication.

**Note:** A Spring Boot application is only vulnerable when both the Actuator and Spring Security dependencies are on the classpath. The application must have a custom endpoint that requires authentication mapped under the `CloudFoundry` actuator path (such as `/cloudfoundryapplication/admin`). This specific misconfiguration of placing application endpoints under the infrastructure actuator namespace is the key prerequisite for this vulnerability to manifest.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4914
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Boot is vulnerable to authentication bypass due to improper handling of endpoint request matchers in the `EndpointRequest` component. This could allow an attacker to access protected application endpoints without proper authentication, potentially exposing sensitive functionality or data.

An application is only vulnerable when it combines Spring Boot Actuator with a specific configuration pattern: the application must have the Actuator dependency on its classpath and configure a custom health group (e.g., "mygroup") using properties like `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.include`. This health group must then be exposed under an additional path on the main server using a configuration such as `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.additional-path=server:/healthz`. The vulnerability becomes exploitable when the application also defines an endpoint requiring authentication that is located under a subpath of this additional health path (e.g., `/healthz/admin`). In this scenario, the flawed path matching logic causes the health group matcher to incorrectly match the authentication-protected endpoint, potentially allowing unauthenticated access to what should be a secured resource.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in:
* [**3.5.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v3.5.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit
* [**4.0.4**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v4.0.4) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit
* [**4.1.0-M3**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v4.1.0-M3) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7607
allVulnerabilities.description: Spring Framework is vulnerable to denial-of-service (DoS) due to a missing existence check in the `FileSystemResource.isReadable()` method used during static resource resolution. When serving static resources from the file system on Windows, the `isReadable()` method called `File.canRead()` without first verifying that the file exists. On Windows, `canRead()` performs expensive security descriptor lookups even for non-existent files, causing these calls to be slow to return. This could allow an attacker to send a high volume of crafted requests for non-existent static resources, keeping HTTP connections occupied and eventually exhausting available connections, resulting in a denial-of-service condition for legitimate users.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.18**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.18) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/684b1e8a4b3df9589f3e9abb8c09a5e98a47e4b5) commit and in [**7.0.7**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.7) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/e977bd7bf0efe8341a03de025ee6ac376e6a57c8) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7608
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring framework contains a cache poisoning vulnerability due to how static resources are resolved when using Spring MVD or Spring Webflux. A remote attacker could exploit this flaw by tricking a victim into interacting with poisoned content, which could lead to a denial-of-service (DoS) and impacts to application performance.

To be vulnerable to this flaw, an application must meet the following conditions:

* the application is using Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux,
* the application is configuring the resource chain support with caching enabled,
* the application adds support for encoded resources resolution,
* the resource cache must be empty when the attacker has access to the application.
allVulnerabilities.solution:

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4886
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework MVC and WebFlux are vulnerable to stream corruption due to improper handling of server-sent events (SSE).

An application is vulnerable when using Spring MVC's `SseEmitter` or Spring WebFlux's `ServerSentEvent` to send Server-Sent Events (SSE) to clients, and an attacker has control over values used in the SSE id or event fields. By injecting newline characters (`\n`, `\r`, or `\r\n`) into these fields, an attacker can break out of the intended SSE field and inject arbitrary SSE events (such as malicious data:, event:, or id: fields) into the stream. Plain text strings in the data field containing CR/CRLF characters are vulnerable but JSON data would be safe in the datafield as the JSON parser would would escape all newline variants properly.
This could allow the attacker to corrupt the SSE stream received by other users, potentially altering application state or presenting false information to victims.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.17**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.17) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/8dc888e1b8190da93d0864c437ae945eaf06a8e9) commit, and [**7.0.6**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.6) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/6e9758700a4946be1dca85ca937ef2603e291301) commit.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7849
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Security is vulnerable to an information disclosure issue when `DaoAuthenticationProvider` is in use as a result of how timing attack defenses can potentially be bypassed.

Specifically, where an application is using the `UserDetails#isEnabled`, `#isAccountNonExpired` or `#isAccountNonLocked` user attributes, defenses can be bypassed for users who are disabled, expired or locked. Authentication can take different lengths of time to complete depending on user account state, which leaks some user attributes.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**7.0.5**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases/tag/7.0.5) and in [**6.5.10**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases/tag/6.5.10) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/commit/a317a3d8663995daa2bc91593bb88ae2da21ea52) commit.

The latest stable releases are available [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases).

In the official [advisory](https://spring.io/security/cve-2026-22746), the vendor has provided the following advice regarding the fixes for this issue:
> Note that this version also introduces a setter `DaoAuthenticationProvider#setAlwaysPerformAdditionalChecksOnUser`. In the event that this upgrade causes you trouble, you can set this value to `false`.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7845
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Security is vulnerable to a time-of-check time-of-use (TOCTOU) race condition due to improper handling of token deletion in the `JdbcOneTimeTokenService` component. This could allow an attacker to consume a one-time token multiple times, potentially bypassing authentication mechanisms.

**Note:** The default `InMemoryOneTimeTokenService` is thread-safe and not affected by this vulnerability.
allVulnerabilities.solution:

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7851
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Security is susceptible to a security misconfiguration issue when using `NimbusJwtDecoder#withIssuerLocation` or `NimbusReactiveJwtDecoder#withIssuerLocation` to configure JWT decoding.

By default, the decoder only verifies the cryptographic signature of JWTs without validating the issuer, unless developers explicitly add an `OAuth2TokenValidator<Jwt>` by calling `setJwtValidator()`. This creates a dangerous security gap where applications may unknowingly accept JWTs from any source as long as they are properly signed.

This default state could lead to authentication bypass scenarios where an attacker sends fraudulent tokens to an affected application wherein they are accepted because the application only checks if the signature is valid, not whether the token came from the trusted authorization server.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7607
allVulnerabilities.description: Spring Framework is vulnerable to denial-of-service (DoS) due to a missing existence check in the `FileSystemResource.isReadable()` method used during static resource resolution. When serving static resources from the file system on Windows, the `isReadable()` method called `File.canRead()` without first verifying that the file exists. On Windows, `canRead()` performs expensive security descriptor lookups even for non-existent files, causing these calls to be slow to return. This could allow an attacker to send a high volume of crafted requests for non-existent static resources, keeping HTTP connections occupied and eventually exhausting available connections, resulting in a denial-of-service condition for legitimate users.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2025-13947

An attacker could trick a victim user into viewing crafted web content that sends requests to an affected application and abuses improper state management to bypass security measures and send unauthorized messages on the victim user's behalf.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7608

Spring framework contains a cache poisoning vulnerability due to how static resources are resolved when using Spring MVD or Spring Webflux. A remote attacker could exploit this flaw by tricking a victim into interacting with poisoned content, which could lead to a denial-of-service (DoS) and impacts to application performance.

To be vulnerable to this flaw, an application must meet the following conditions:

* the application is using Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux,
* the application is configuring the resource chain support with caching enabled,
* the application adds support for encoded resources resolution,
* the resource cache must be empty when the attacker has access to the application.

allVulnerabilities.description:

An application is only vulnerable when it combines Spring Boot Actuator with a specific configuration pattern: the application must have the Actuator dependency on its classpath and configure a custom health group (e.g., "mygroup") using properties like `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.include`. This health group must then be exposed under an additional path on the main server using a configuration such as `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.additional-path=server:/healthz`. The vulnerability becomes exploitable when the application also defines an endpoint requiring authentication that is located under a subpath of this additional health path (e.g., `/healthz/admin`). In this scenario, the flawed path matching logic causes the health group matcher to incorrectly match the authentication-protected endpoint, potentially allowing unauthenticated access to what should be a secured resource.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4925
allVulnerabilities.description:

**Note:** A Spring Boot application is only vulnerable when both the Actuator and Spring Security dependencies are on the classpath. The application must have a custom endpoint that requires authentication mapped under the `CloudFoundry` actuator path (such as `/cloudfoundryapplication/admin`). This specific misconfiguration of placing application endpoints under the infrastructure actuator namespace is the key prerequisite for this vulnerability to manifest.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4914
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Boot is vulnerable to authentication bypass due to improper handling of endpoint request matchers in the `EndpointRequest` component. This could allow an attacker to access protected application endpoints without proper authentication, potentially exposing sensitive functionality or data.

An application is only vulnerable when it combines Spring Boot Actuator with a specific configuration pattern: the application must have the Actuator dependency on its classpath and configure a custom health group (e.g., "mygroup") using properties like `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.include`. This health group must then be exposed under an additional path on the main server using a configuration such as `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.additional-path=server:/healthz`. The vulnerability becomes exploitable when the application also defines an endpoint requiring authentication that is located under a subpath of this additional health path (e.g., `/healthz/admin`). In this scenario, the flawed path matching logic causes the health group matcher to incorrectly match the authentication-protected endpoint, potentially allowing unauthenticated access to what should be a secured resource.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in:
* [**3.5.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v3.5.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit

* [**4.1.0-M3**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v4.1.0-M3) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7607
allVulnerabilities.description: Spring Framework is vulnerable to denial-of-service (DoS) due to a missing existence check in the `FileSystemResource.isReadable()` method used during static resource resolution. When serving static resources from the file system on Windows, the `isReadable()` method called `File.canRead()` without first verifying that the file exists. On Windows, `canRead()` performs expensive security descriptor lookups even for non-existent files, causing these calls to be slow to return. This could allow an attacker to send a high volume of crafted requests for non-existent static resources, keeping HTTP connections occupied and eventually exhausting available connections, resulting in a denial-of-service condition for legitimate users.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.18**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.18) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/684b1e8a4b3df9589f3e9abb8c09a5e98a47e4b5) commit and in [**7.0.7**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.7) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/e977bd7bf0efe8341a03de025ee6ac376e6a57c8) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7608
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring framework contains a cache poisoning vulnerability due to how static resources are resolved when using Spring MVD or Spring Webflux. A remote attacker could exploit this flaw by tricking a victim into interacting with poisoned content, which could lead to a denial-of-service (DoS) and impacts to application performance.

To be vulnerable to this flaw, an application must meet the following conditions:

* the application is using Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux,
* the application is configuring the resource chain support with caching enabled,
* the application adds support for encoded resources resolution,
* the resource cache must be empty when the attacker has access to the application.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.18**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.18) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/e607f1c30fd99e146cdaf186c56d1f1275e3fbc1) commit, and [**7.0.7**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.7) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/0b2e3900b9ce57873f3393ba9e026cb58d8721ba) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4886
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework MVC and WebFlux are vulnerable to stream corruption due to improper handling of server-sent events (SSE).

An application is vulnerable when using Spring MVC's `SseEmitter` or Spring WebFlux's `ServerSentEvent` to send Server-Sent Events (SSE) to clients, and an attacker has control over values used in the SSE id or event fields. By injecting newline characters (`\n`, `\r`, or `\r\n`) into these fields, an attacker can break out of the intended SSE field and inject arbitrary SSE events (such as malicious data:, event:, or id: fields) into the stream. Plain text strings in the data field containing CR/CRLF characters are vulnerable but JSON data would be safe in the datafield as the JSON parser would would escape all newline variants properly.
This could allow the attacker to corrupt the SSE stream received by other users, potentially altering application state or presenting false information to victims.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.17**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.17) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/8dc888e1b8190da93d0864c437ae945eaf06a8e9) commit, and [**7.0.6**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.6) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/6e9758700a4946be1dca85ca937ef2603e291301) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-40477
allVulnerabilities.description: Thymeleaf is a server-side Java template engine for web and standalone environments. Versions 3.1.3.RELEASE and prior contain a security bypass vulnerability in the expression execution mechanisms. Although the library provides mechanisms to prevent expression injection, it fails to properly restrict the scope of accessible objects, allowing specific potentially sensitive objects to be reached from within a template. If an application developer passes unvalidated user input directly to the template engine, an unauthenticated remote attacker can bypass the library's protections to achieve Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI). This issue has ben fixed in version 3.1.4.RELEASE.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2025-13947
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework is vulnerable to a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) issue due to the presence of a security bypass flaw in the STOMP protocol functionality. Applications that use STOMP over WebSocket are affected by this issue.

An attacker could trick a victim user into viewing crafted web content that sends requests to an affected application and abuses improper state management to bypass security measures and send unauthorized messages on the victim user's behalf.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in Spring Framework OSS [**7.0.0-RC1**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.0-RC1) and [**6.2.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/c88bfc54c9256e3c07511aa303be2b136c03e0e2) commit.

The latest stable releases are available [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7608
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring framework contains a cache poisoning vulnerability due to how static resources are resolved when using Spring MVD or Spring Webflux. A remote attacker could exploit this flaw by tricking a victim into interacting with poisoned content, which could lead to a denial-of-service (DoS) and impacts to application performance.

To be vulnerable to this flaw, an application must meet the following conditions:

* the application is using Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux,
* the application is configuring the resource chain support with caching enabled,
* the application adds support for encoded resources resolution,
* the resource cache must be empty when the attacker has access to the application.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.18**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.18) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/e607f1c30fd99e146cdaf186c56d1f1275e3fbc1) commit, and [**7.0.7**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.7) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/0b2e3900b9ce57873f3393ba9e026cb58d8721ba) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4886
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework MVC and WebFlux are vulnerable to stream corruption due to improper handling of server-sent events (SSE).

An application is vulnerable when using Spring MVC's `SseEmitter` or Spring WebFlux's `ServerSentEvent` to send Server-Sent Events (SSE) to clients, and an attacker has control over values used in the SSE id or event fields. By injecting newline characters (`\n`, `\r`, or `\r\n`) into these fields, an attacker can break out of the intended SSE field and inject arbitrary SSE events (such as malicious data:, event:, or id: fields) into the stream. Plain text strings in the data field containing CR/CRLF characters are vulnerable but JSON data would be safe in the datafield as the JSON parser would would escape all newline variants properly.
This could allow the attacker to corrupt the SSE stream received by other users, potentially altering application state or presenting false information to victims.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.17**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.17) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/8dc888e1b8190da93d0864c437ae945eaf06a8e9) commit, and [**7.0.6**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.6) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/6e9758700a4946be1dca85ca937ef2603e291301) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4873

**Note**: In order for the application to be vulnerable it must have a mapping for `/**` that results in view rendering where the view name is not explicitly specified.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2025-14740
allVulnerabilities.description:
Apache Tomcat is vulnerable to a denial-of-service (DoS) issue due to how cleanup operations of temporary files can be delayed under certain circumstances.

An attacker could upload crafted multipart upload content that causes an error to occur during processing of the upload. Doing so could then cause excessive space to be consumed by temporary copies of uploaded parts that are not yet cleaned up by the JVM garbage collection process. Depending on settings, application memory usage and load, it could be possible for the space to be filled faster than it is cleared, leading to a denial-of-service (DoS).
allVulnerabilities.solution:

* [**9.0.110**](https://github.com/apache/tomcat/releases/tag/9.0.110) by [this](https://github.com/apache/tomcat/commit/afa422bd7c) commit,

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2025-14740
allVulnerabilities.description:
Apache Tomcat is vulnerable to a denial-of-service (DoS) issue due to how cleanup operations of temporary files can be delayed under certain circumstances.

An attacker could upload crafted multipart upload content that causes an error to occur during processing of the upload. Doing so could then cause excessive space to be consumed by temporary copies of uploaded parts that are not yet cleaned up by the JVM garbage collection process. Depending on settings, application memory usage and load, it could be possible for the space to be filled faster than it is cleared, leading to a denial-of-service (DoS).
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in:
* [**9.0.110**](https://github.com/apache/tomcat/releases/tag/9.0.110) by [this](https://github.com/apache/tomcat/commit/afa422bd7c) commit,
* [**10.1.47**](https://github.com/apache/tomcat/releases/tag/10.1.47) by [this](https://github.com/apache/tomcat/commit/af6e918162) commit,
* [**11.0.12**](https://github.com/apache/tomcat/releases/tag/11.0.12) by [this](https://github.com/apache/tomcat/commit/1cdf5f730e) commit,
* **main** branch by [this](https://github.com/apache/tomcat/commit/f0a0021d65) commit.

The latest stable releases are available [here](https://github.com/apache/tomcat/tags).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4914
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Boot is vulnerable to authentication bypass due to improper handling of endpoint request matchers in the `EndpointRequest` component. This could allow an attacker to access protected application endpoints without proper authentication, potentially exposing sensitive functionality or data.

An application is only vulnerable when it combines Spring Boot Actuator with a specific configuration pattern: the application must have the Actuator dependency on its classpath and configure a custom health group (e.g., "mygroup") using properties like `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.include`. This health group must then be exposed under an additional path on the main server using a configuration such as `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.additional-path=server:/healthz`. The vulnerability becomes exploitable when the application also defines an endpoint requiring authentication that is located under a subpath of this additional health path (e.g., `/healthz/admin`). In this scenario, the flawed path matching logic causes the health group matcher to incorrectly match the authentication-protected endpoint, potentially allowing unauthenticated access to what should be a secured resource.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in:
* [**3.5.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v3.5.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4925
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Boot is vulnerable to authentication bypass due to improper handling of requests in the `CloudFoundry` actuator endpoint mapping. This could allow an attacker to access protected application endpoints without proper authentication.

**Note:** A Spring Boot application is only vulnerable when both the Actuator and Spring Security dependencies are on the classpath. The application must have a custom endpoint that requires authentication mapped under the `CloudFoundry` actuator path (such as `/cloudfoundryapplication/admin`). This specific misconfiguration of placing application endpoints under the infrastructure actuator namespace is the key prerequisite for this vulnerability to manifest.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in:
* [**3.5.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v3.5.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/01fbede2b27237616e215fe0df7c294ae47bdd73) commit
* [**4.0.4**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v4.0.4) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/01fbede2b27237616e215fe0df7c294ae47bdd73) commit
* [**4.1.0-M3**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v4.1.0-M3) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/01fbede2b27237616e215fe0df7c294ae47bdd73) commit

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-8090
allVulnerabilities.description: Spring Boot is vulnerable to arbitrary code execution due to improper temporary directory handling when `server.servlet.session.persistent` is set to `true`. This could allow a local attacker to read session information and hijack authenticated users or potentially deploy a gadget chain and execute code as the application's user.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/5ceb1a228932e35cc803d1c1fea68f0f984aaa90) commit in the following versions: 
* [**4.1.0-RC1**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v4.1.0-RC1)

* [**3.5.14**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v3.5.14)

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4914
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Boot is vulnerable to authentication bypass due to improper handling of endpoint request matchers in the `EndpointRequest` component. This could allow an attacker to access protected application endpoints without proper authentication, potentially exposing sensitive functionality or data.

An application is only vulnerable when it combines Spring Boot Actuator with a specific configuration pattern: the application must have the Actuator dependency on its classpath and configure a custom health group (e.g., "mygroup") using properties like `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.include`. This health group must then be exposed under an additional path on the main server using a configuration such as `management.endpoint.health.group.mygroup.additional-path=server:/healthz`. The vulnerability becomes exploitable when the application also defines an endpoint requiring authentication that is located under a subpath of this additional health path (e.g., `/healthz/admin`). In this scenario, the flawed path matching logic causes the health group matcher to incorrectly match the authentication-protected endpoint, potentially allowing unauthenticated access to what should be a secured resource.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in:
* [**3.5.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v3.5.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit
* [**4.0.4**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v4.0.4) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit
* [**4.1.0-M3**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/releases/tag/v4.1.0-M3) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot/commit/1f2ea4a6db03eb1d666a174a525f2bff658b8296) commit

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-boot).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-8087

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7607
allVulnerabilities.description: Spring Framework is vulnerable to denial-of-service (DoS) due to a missing existence check in the `FileSystemResource.isReadable()` method used during static resource resolution. When serving static resources from the file system on Windows, the `isReadable()` method called `File.canRead()` without first verifying that the file exists. On Windows, `canRead()` performs expensive security descriptor lookups even for non-existent files, causing these calls to be slow to return. This could allow an attacker to send a high volume of crafted requests for non-existent static resources, keeping HTTP connections occupied and eventually exhausting available connections, resulting in a denial-of-service condition for legitimate users.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.18**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.18) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/684b1e8a4b3df9589f3e9abb8c09a5e98a47e4b5) commit and in [**7.0.7**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.7) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/e977bd7bf0efe8341a03de025ee6ac376e6a57c8) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2025-13947
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework is vulnerable to a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) issue due to the presence of a security bypass flaw in the STOMP protocol functionality. Applications that use STOMP over WebSocket are affected by this issue.

An attacker could trick a victim user into viewing crafted web content that sends requests to an affected application and abuses improper state management to bypass security measures and send unauthorized messages on the victim user's behalf.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in Spring Framework OSS [**7.0.0-RC1**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.0-RC1) and [**6.2.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/c88bfc54c9256e3c07511aa303be2b136c03e0e2) commit.

The latest stable releases are available [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7608
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring framework contains a cache poisoning vulnerability due to how static resources are resolved when using Spring MVD or Spring Webflux. A remote attacker could exploit this flaw by tricking a victim into interacting with poisoned content, which could lead to a denial-of-service (DoS) and impacts to application performance.

To be vulnerable to this flaw, an application must meet the following conditions:

* the application is using Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux,
* the application is configuring the resource chain support with caching enabled,
* the application adds support for encoded resources resolution,
* the resource cache must be empty when the attacker has access to the application.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.18**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.18) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/e607f1c30fd99e146cdaf186c56d1f1275e3fbc1) commit, and [**7.0.7**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.7) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/0b2e3900b9ce57873f3393ba9e026cb58d8721ba) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4886
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework MVC and WebFlux are vulnerable to stream corruption due to improper handling of server-sent events (SSE).

An application is vulnerable when using Spring MVC's `SseEmitter` or Spring WebFlux's `ServerSentEvent` to send Server-Sent Events (SSE) to clients, and an attacker has control over values used in the SSE id or event fields. By injecting newline characters (`\n`, `\r`, or `\r\n`) into these fields, an attacker can break out of the intended SSE field and inject arbitrary SSE events (such as malicious data:, event:, or id: fields) into the stream. Plain text strings in the data field containing CR/CRLF characters are vulnerable but JSON data would be safe in the datafield as the JSON parser would would escape all newline variants properly.
This could allow the attacker to corrupt the SSE stream received by other users, potentially altering application state or presenting false information to victims.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.17**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.17) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/8dc888e1b8190da93d0864c437ae945eaf06a8e9) commit, and [**7.0.6**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.6) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/6e9758700a4946be1dca85ca937ef2603e291301) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4873
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework is vulnerable to information disclosure due to improper resource handling in `ScriptTemplateView` within Spring MVC and Spring WebFlux. This could allow an attacker to access content from files outside the configured locations for script template views.

**Note**: In order for the application to be vulnerable it must have a mapping for `/**` that results in view rendering where the view name is not explicitly specified.
allVulnerabilities.solution:

By injecting newline characters (`\n`, `\r`, or `\r\n`) into these fields, an attacker can break out of the intended SSE field and inject arbitrary SSE events (such as malicious data:, event:, or id: fields) into the stream. Plain text strings in the data field containing CR/CRLF characters are vulnerable but JSON data would be safe in the datafield as the JSON parser would would escape all newline variants properly.
This could allow the attacker to corrupt the SSE stream received by other users, potentially altering application state or presenting false information to victims.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.17**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.17) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/8dc888e1b8190da93d0864c437ae945eaf06a8e9) commit, and [**7.0.6**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.6) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/6e9758700a4946be1dca85ca937ef2603e291301) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7607
allVulnerabilities.description: Spring Framework is vulnerable to denial-of-service (DoS) due to a missing existence check in the `FileSystemResource.isReadable()` method used during static resource resolution. When serving static resources from the file system on Windows, the `isReadable()` method called `File.canRead()` without first verifying that the file exists. On Windows, `canRead()` performs expensive security descriptor lookups even for non-existent files, causing these calls to be slow to return. This could allow an attacker to send a high volume of crafted requests for non-existent static resources, keeping HTTP connections occupied and eventually exhausting available connections, resulting in a denial-of-service condition for legitimate users.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.18**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.18) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/684b1e8a4b3df9589f3e9abb8c09a5e98a47e4b5) commit and in [**7.0.7**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.7) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/e977bd7bf0efe8341a03de025ee6ac376e6a57c8) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2025-13947
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Framework is vulnerable to a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) issue due to the presence of a security bypass flaw in the STOMP protocol functionality. Applications that use STOMP over WebSocket are affected by this issue.

An attacker could trick a victim user into viewing crafted web content that sends requests to an affected application and abuses improper state management to bypass security measures and send unauthorized messages on the victim user's behalf.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in Spring Framework OSS [**7.0.0-RC1**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.0-RC1) and [**6.2.12**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.12) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/c88bfc54c9256e3c07511aa303be2b136c03e0e2) commit.

The latest stable releases are available [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7608
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring framework contains a cache poisoning vulnerability due to how static resources are resolved when using Spring MVD or Spring Webflux. A remote attacker could exploit this flaw by tricking a victim into interacting with poisoned content, which could lead to a denial-of-service (DoS) and impacts to application performance.

To be vulnerable to this flaw, an application must meet the following conditions:

* the application is using Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux,
* the application is configuring the resource chain support with caching enabled,
* the application adds support for encoded resources resolution,
* the resource cache must be empty when the attacker has access to the application.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**6.2.18**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v6.2.18) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/e607f1c30fd99e146cdaf186c56d1f1275e3fbc1) commit, and [**7.0.7**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/releases/tag/v7.0.7) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework/commit/0b2e3900b9ce57873f3393ba9e026cb58d8721ba) commit.

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-framework).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-6606
allVulnerabilities.description:
FasterXML Jackson Core (`jackson-core`) is vulnerable to a denial-of-service (DoS) issue due to how the asynchronous parsing path in the `NonBlockingUtf8JsonParserBase` functionality does not sufficiently validate number length. This issue causes the `maxNumberLength` constraint defined in `StreamReadConstraints` to be bypassed.

This bypass issue could allow an attacker to trigger excessive memory allocation or CPU resource exhaustion by sending crafted JSON content that includes arbitrarily long numbers to an affected application that uses the asynchronous parser API.

**Note**: This issue, **BDSA-2026-6606**, is a variant of the previously reported number length validation flaw that affected synchronous code paths (**BDSA-2022-4307**).
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed by [this](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-core/commit/b0c428e6f993e1b5ece5c1c3cb2523e887cd52cf) commit in:
* jackson-core [**2.18.6**](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-core/releases/tag/jackson-core-2.18.6),
* jackson-core [**2.21.1**](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-core/releases/tag/jackson-core-2.21.1),
* jackson-core [**3.1.0**](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-core/releases/tag/jackson-core-3.1.0).

The latest stable releases are available [here](https://github.com/FasterXML/jackson-core/tags).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7849
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Security is vulnerable to an information disclosure issue when `DaoAuthenticationProvider` is in use as a result of how timing attack defenses can potentially be bypassed.

Specifically, where an application is using the `UserDetails#isEnabled`, `#isAccountNonExpired` or `#isAccountNonLocked` user attributes, defenses can be bypassed for users who are disabled, expired or locked. Authentication can take different lengths of time to complete depending on user account state, which leaks some user attributes.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed in [**7.0.5**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases/tag/7.0.5) and in [**6.5.10**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases/tag/6.5.10) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/commit/a317a3d8663995daa2bc91593bb88ae2da21ea52) commit.

The latest stable releases are available [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases).

In the official [advisory](https://spring.io/security/cve-2026-22746), the vendor has provided the following advice regarding the fixes for this issue:
> Note that this version also introduces a setter `DaoAuthenticationProvider#setAlwaysPerformAdditionalChecksOnUser`. In the event that this upgrade causes you trouble, you can set this value to `false`.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7845
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Security is vulnerable to a time-of-check time-of-use (TOCTOU) race condition due to improper handling of token deletion in the `JdbcOneTimeTokenService` component. This could allow an attacker to consume a one-time token multiple times, potentially bypassing authentication mechanisms.

**Note:** The default `InMemoryOneTimeTokenService` is thread-safe and not affected by this vulnerability.
allVulnerabilities.solution:
Fixed by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/commit/4187af38b251fc97fdf9949f7869618111e6e261) commit in the following:
* [**6.5.10**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases/tag/6.5.10),
* [**7.0.5**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases/tag/7.0.5),
* [**7.1.0-rc1**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases/tag/7.1.0-RC1).

The latest stable releases can be found [here](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security).

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-7851
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Security is susceptible to a security misconfiguration issue when using `NimbusJwtDecoder#withIssuerLocation` or `NimbusReactiveJwtDecoder#withIssuerLocation` to configure JWT decoding.

By default, the decoder only verifies the cryptographic signature of JWTs without validating the issuer, unless developers explicitly add an `OAuth2TokenValidator<Jwt>` by calling `setJwtValidator()`. This creates a dangerous security gap where applications may unknowingly accept JWTs from any source as long as they are properly signed.

This default state could lead to authentication bypass scenarios where an attacker sends fraudulent tokens to an affected application wherein they are accepted because the application only checks if the signature is valid, not whether the token came from the trusted authorization server.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4920
allVulnerabilities.description: Spring Security is vulnerable to improper handling of HTTP response headers due to missing overrides in the `OnCommittedResponseWrapper` component. This could allow an attacker to cause the response body to be committed before the response headers are written, potentially leading to inconsistent or incomplete HTTP responses.
allVulnerabilities.solution:

* [**7.1.0-m3**](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/releases/tag/7.1.0-M3) by [this](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/commit/1dae9aa459436e0bb1a95701ed0d31e12be7788a) commit.

allVulnerabilities.name: BDSA-2026-4925
allVulnerabilities.description:
Spring Boot is vulnerable to authentication bypass due to improper handling of requests in the `CloudFoundry` actuator endpoint mapping. This could allow an attacker to access protected application endpoints without proper authentication.

**Note:** A Spring Boot application is only vulnerable when both the Actuator and Spring Security dependencies are on the classpath. The application must have a custom endpoint that requires authentication mapped under the `CloudFoundry` actuator path (such as `/cloudfoundryapplication/admin`). This specific misconfiguration of placing application endpoints under the infrastructure actuator namespace is the key prerequisite for this vulnerability to manifest.
